profile.name: Daniel
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to take the lead on big-picture planning and is the main point of contact in meetings.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing retirement accounts, taxes, and cash flow, and can follow tradeoffs, but prefers the advisor to translate jargon into plain English.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk in practice: willing to stay invested for long-term goals but gets uneasy when markets drop and wants a clear plan for withdrawals and a cash buffer before agreeing to changes.
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; often speaks in specifics and will reference exact figures when he has them. Uses occasional hedging like “I think” or “if I’m remembering right,” but generally tries to be precise and will ask for confirmation.
emotional_tendencies: relieved when there is a written plan
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and checklist-driven; prefers to compare a couple of options, understand pros/cons, then decide after sleeping on it.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share information and listen, but trust increases only after the advisor demonstrates follow-through and clarity on fees and next steps.
internal_biases: loss aversion (dislikes seeing account values drop)
status quo bias (prefers small, incremental changes)
hidden_concerns: worries about being a burden if health declines (he is a smoker and describes his health as fair)
concerned about taxes and making a mistake that triggers unnecessary tax costs

profile.name: Jennifer
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on day-to-day spending comfort and quality of life, and weighs in strongly on what feels “safe,” even if Daniel leads the meeting.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the basics of budgeting and account types, but prefers round numbers and may mix up terms (gross vs. net, balance vs. payment) until clarified.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative in day-to-day behavior: prefers predictability and liquidity, asks “what happens if…” questions, and may push to keep more in cash even when she agrees with a moderate overall plan.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; may interrupt with practical questions. Uses phrases like “roughly,” “around,” and “I’m not totally sure,” and appreciates summaries and confirmation in plain language.
emotional_tendencies: stress spikes when bills or debt are discussed
calms down with clear monthly numbers and reassurance
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking; wants to hear Daniel’s view and the advisor’s recommendation, then aligns once she feels the plan won’t disrupt their routine.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Warm but verifying: tends to like people quickly, yet wants the advisor to repeat key points and confirm details in writing before she feels fully comfortable.
internal_biases: availability bias (recent headlines can influence her comfort level)
hidden_concerns: worries about outliving assets and needing stable income despite being retired
concerned about health risks over time (she is also a smoker, though she reports good health)
